skill.name: tophub-defou-stanley-combo
skill.description: Fetches TopHub trends, selects high-potential topics, and generates Simplified Chinese articles in Defou x Stanley styles.
skill.summary: Generate Simplified Chinese content drafts from TopHub trends.
skill.author: wangdefou-dev

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives caused by JavaScript template literals, Markdown backticks, metadata, and report text inside EVALUATION.json. The confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: the script reads .env credentials, performs external network requests, allows a configurable Anthropic base URL, and writes generated Markdown files. No prompt injection instructions or confirmed malicious intent were found.

medium_findings.title: Configurable LLM Endpoint Can Redirect API Traffic
medium_findings.description: The script reads ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL from the environment and passes it to the Anthropic client with the API key. This is useful for proxies, but a malicious or mistaken .env value could send prompts and credentials to a non-Anthropic endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly wires an environment-controlled base URL into the SDK client. Legitimate proxy use is possible, so this is a configuration risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Network Calls Send Trend Data to an LLM
medium_findings.description: The skill fetches public TopHub trend data and sends selected topics to the Anthropic API for topic selection and content generation. This is expected behavior, but users should understand the external data flow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicit and match the documented workflow. The risk is disclosure to external services, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Web Topics Are Inserted Into Prompts
medium_findings.description: TopHub titles and metadata are embedded into model prompts. A malicious trend title could influence generated content, although the script does not execute model output as code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt includes externally sourced text, which creates prompt-injection and content-integrity risk. Impact is limited because outputs are written as Markdown only.

low_findings.title: Generated Markdown Written to Local Output Directory
low_findings.description: The script creates an output directory and writes generated Markdown files. The title is sanitized before use in the filename, and no user-controlled path is passed to writeFileSync.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is real and expected for the skill. The path is constrained to the configured output directory and the filename is sanitized.

low_findings.title: .env File Access Is Configuration Loading
low_findings.description: The skill resolves a project .env path and loads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, and MOCK_MODE. No hardcoded secret was found, and the access is visible configuration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dotenv and process.env usage is explicit and standard. It remains a risk factor because the skill handles credentials.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Template Literals
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution findings in index.ts and SKILL.md are JavaScript template strings or Markdown formatting. No child_process, eval, spawn, or shell invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is visible string construction and documentation. Targeted search found no executable shell API usage.

low_findings.title: EVALUATION.json Findings Are Report Text
low_findings.description: The EVALUATION.json file contains prior audit report strings and code examples. The scanner flagged quoted snippets inside JSON as if they were executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is JSON report content, not executable TypeScript or shell. The flagged lines are nested strings describing earlier findings.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Flags Are Metadata Noise
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and high-entropy alerts point to YAML front matter, Chinese text, and prior report strings. No cryptographic algorithm, packed payload, or encrypted content was found in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are metadata, prose, or report entries, not crypto code. The surrounding files are readable text rather than obfuscated payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Controlled API Base URL
dangerous_patterns.description: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL controls where the Anthropic SDK sends requests. Users should leave it unset or verify the endpoint before running the skill.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint override is implemented directly. The code does not prove malicious intent, but the pattern can expose credentials if configured unsafely.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Use With External Network Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and uses it for outbound LLM requests. This should be disclosed because the skill depends on a user-provided secret.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The key is read from process.env and passed into the SDK. This is normal API use, but it is still a real credential-handling pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Content Saved to Disk
dangerous_patterns.description: The script writes model-generated Markdown to outputs/defou-stanley-posts. Users should review files before publishing or executing anything copied from them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path and output format are clear in the code. The main concern is downstream trust in generated content.

content.value_statement: Content teams need fast topic discovery and draft generation from live trends. This skill fetches TopHub items, selects promising topics, and creates structured Simplified Chinese drafts.
actual_capabilities: Fetches live items from the TopHub hot list.
Parses ranking, title, source, link, and heat metadata.
Uses Claude to select ten topics with strong content potential.
Generates three Simplified Chinese draft styles for each selected topic.
Creates hooks, routing guidance, scores, and publishing advice.
Saves generated drafts as Markdown files in an outputs directory.
limitations: Requires an Anthropic API key unless mock mode is enabled.
Designed for Simplified Chinese content and Chinese trend sources.
Generated claims and advice require human review before publishing.
TopHub availability and page structure can affect scraping results.

use_cases.title: Plan Daily Social Posts
use_cases.description: Turn current TopHub topics into draft posts with hooks and article angles.

use_cases.title: Develop Opinion Drafts
use_cases.description: Explore sharp Chinese article angles from trending social and career topics.

use_cases.title: Prepare Content Pipelines
use_cases.description: Batch-generate Markdown drafts for review, editing, and editorial planning.

prompt_templates.title: Run Default Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the TopHub Defou Stanley combo workflow and generate Markdown drafts for the default ten selected topics.

prompt_templates.title: Review Selected Topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch TopHub trends, select ten topics, and explain why each topic fits the Defou x Stanley content style.

prompt_templates.title: Focus Topic Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the workflow and prioritize topics about careers, wealth, relationships, and social psychology.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Output Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the drafts, then review each draft for factual risk, weak claims, tone issues, and publishing readiness.

output: A ranked topic shortlist with reasons for selection.
Three draft versions for each selected topic.
Markdown files saved under the configured output folder.

output_examples.input: Generate drafts focused on workplace topics.
output: Workplace-related topics are favored during selection.
Each draft includes hooks, routing, scoring, and publishing advice.
The result is ready for editor review, not automatic publication.

best_practices: Verify ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL before running the workflow.
Review generated facts, claims, and tone before publishing.
Use mock mode when testing setup or output paths.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated drafts without human editing.
Do not use an untrusted API base URL with real credentials.
Do not assume scraped trend titles are accurate or safe prompts.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates Simplified Chinese Markdown drafts from TopHub trend topics.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It fetches TopHub pages and calls the Anthropic API for generation.

faq.question: Does it read secrets?
faq.answer: It reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the environment or project .env file.

faq.question: Where are outputs saved?
faq.answer: The script writes Markdown files under outputs/defou-stanley-posts.

faq.question: Is the output ready to publish?
faq.answer: No. Review factual accuracy, tone, and brand fit before publishing.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The workflow uses credentials, network calls, external web text, and local file writes.
